title: The tea I share with my grandmother
summary: Describe a small ritual of drinking tea with your grandmother every week.
topic: The tea I share with my grandmother
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about drinking tea with your grandmother. Describe when you visit, the tea, and what you talk about. Use present simple.
hintStarters: Every Saturday afternoon I visit my grandmother because…
She makes tea in a small clay pot. The tea is…
sampleText: Every Saturday afternoon, I visit my grandmother in Hue. She lives in a small wooden house with a long porch. She is eighty-one years old, but she still makes tea by hand. She uses a small clay pot and a wooden tray. She heats the water in a metal kettle on a small stove. The tea is green and very fresh. She pours it into two small cups. We sit on a low bench. She also brings a plate of sweet rice cake. We do not speak much in the first ten minutes. We listen to the birds outside. Then she tells me old stories about my mother as a child. Her hands are small and wrinkled, but they move with care. I help her wash the cups after we drink. I think this quiet afternoon is the most important hour of my week. The tea is simple, but the time with my grandmother is not.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'but' and 'still' for contrast
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'still' nghĩa là 'vẫn'. Kết hợp với 'but' để tả sự kiên trì: 'She is eighty-one years old, but she still makes tea by hand'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'still' means 'continuing now'. Combine with 'but' to show that something continues despite an obstacle: 'She is eighty-one years old, but she still makes tea by hand'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Superlative 'the most important'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Tính từ dài (hai âm tiết trở lên) dùng 'the most + tính từ': 'the most important hour of my week'. Không dùng -est cho tính từ dài.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Long adjectives (two or more syllables) take 'the most + adjective': 'the most important hour of my week'. Don't add -est to long adjectives.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi chiều thứ Bảy, tôi đến thăm bà tôi ở Huế. Bà sống trong một ngôi nhà gỗ nhỏ có hiên dài. Bà tám mươi mốt tuổi, nhưng bà vẫn pha trà bằng tay. Bà dùng một ấm đất nhỏ và một khay gỗ. Bà đun nước trong một ấm kim loại trên bếp nhỏ. Trà xanh và rất tươi. Bà rót trà vào hai chén nhỏ. Chúng tôi ngồi trên một chiếc ghế thấp. Bà cũng mang ra một đĩa bánh nếp ngọt. Mười phút đầu chúng tôi không nói nhiều. Chúng tôi lắng nghe tiếng chim bên ngoài. Sau đó bà kể tôi nghe những câu chuyện xưa về mẹ tôi lúc còn bé. Đôi tay bà nhỏ và nhăn nheo, nhưng chúng cử động cẩn thận. Tôi giúp bà rửa chén sau khi uống. Tôi nghĩ buổi chiều yên tĩnh này là một giờ quan trọng nhất trong tuần của tôi. Trà thì đơn giản, nhưng thời gian bên bà thì không.

mcQuestions.prompt: How often does the writer visit her grandmother?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Every Saturday afternoon, I visit my grandmother'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the frequency in the first sentence.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'She is eighty-one years old'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does she use to make the tea?
options: A small clay pot and a wooden tray
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'She uses a small clay pot and a wooden tray'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the tools for making tea.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The tea is green and very fresh'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What food does the grandmother bring?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'a plate of sweet rice cake'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the food on the plate.

mcQuestions.prompt: What do they do in the first ten minutes?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'We listen to the birds outside'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the grandmother tell stories about?
options: The writer's mother as a child
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'old stories about my mother as a child'.

options: She still is eighty-one years old.
She is eighty-one years old, but she still makes tea by hand.
She makes still tea by hand.
Still she makes tea by hand.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'still' goes before the main verb: 'she still makes'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the natural English word order with 'still'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which superlative form is correct for the long adjective 'important'?
What does the writer think about the afternoon?
options: It is the most important hour of the week
It is too short to enjoy
It is the same as any other day
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'this quiet afternoon is the most important hour of my week'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the writer's opinion sentence.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small rituals like a weekly tea make a relationship stronger than big presents? What changes when we keep doing the same simple thing for years?
